Approach: Solution:

View C++ Code (8108 chars)
#include <iostream>
#include <vector>
#include <algorithm>
#include <queue>
#include <map>
#include <set>
#include <tuple> // For std::tie

const long long INF = 1e18; // Use a large enough value for infinity

// Function to reconstruct path from u to v using next_node matrix
void get_path_edges(int u, int v, const std::vector<std::vector<int>>& next_node, std::set<std::pair<int, int>>& result_edges) {
    if (u == v) return;
    while (u != v) {
        int next = next_node[u][v];
        if (next == -1) { // No path or issue with reconstruction
            // This should ideally not happen if paths exist
            break;
        }
        result_edges.insert({std::min(u, next), std::max(u, next)});
        u = next;
    }
}

int main() {
    std::ios_base::sync_with_stdio(false);
    std::cin.tie(NULL);

    int num_vertices, num_edges, num_terminals;
    std::cin >> num_vertices >> num_edges >> num_terminals;

    std::vector<std::vector<long long>> adj_matrix(num_vertices, std::vector<long long>(num_vertices, INF));
    std::vector<std::vector<int>> next_node(num_vertices, std::vector<int>(num_vertices, -1)); // For path reconstruction in Floyd-Warshall

    for (int i = 0; i < num_vertices; ++i) {
        adj_matrix[i][i] = 0;
    }

    for (int i = 0; i < num_edges; ++i) {
        int u, v;
        long long weight;
        std::cin >> u >> v >> weight;
        // For undirected graph, add edges in both directions
        if (weight < adj_matrix[u][v]) { // Only update if new weight is smaller (handling parallel edges)
            adj_matrix[u][v] = weight;
            adj_matrix[v][u] = weight;
            next_node[u][v] = v; // Direct edge, next node from u to v is v
            next_node[v][u] = u; // Direct edge, next node from v to u is u
        }
    }

    std::vector<int> terminals(num_terminals);
    std::map<int, int> terminal_to_idx; // Map terminal vertex to its index in 'terminals' array
    for (int i = 0; i < num_terminals; ++i) {
        std::cin >> terminals[i];
        terminal_to_idx[terminals[i]] = i;
    }

    // Floyd-Warshall to find all-pairs shortest paths
    for (int k = 0; k < num_vertices; ++k) {
        for (int i = 0; i < num_vertices; ++i) {
            for (int j = 0; j < num_vertices; ++j) {
                if (adj_matrix[i][k] != INF && adj_matrix[k][j] != INF) {
                    if (adj_matrix[i][j] > adj_matrix[i][k] + adj_matrix[k][j]) {
                        adj_matrix[i][j] = adj_matrix[i][k] + adj_matrix[k][j];
                        next_node[i][j] = next_node[i][k]; // Shortest path from i to j goes through k, so next node from i is same as next node from i to k
                    }
                }
            }
        }
    }

    // DP state: dp[mask][v] = minimum cost to connect terminals in 'mask' rooted at 'v'
    std::vector<std::vector<long long>> dp(1 << num_terminals, std::vector<long long>(num_vertices, INF));
    // For reconstruction:
    // type: 0 for base (terminal itself), 1 for split (from combining submasks), 2 for path (from extending from another vertex u)
    // value: if split, stores submask1; if path, stores parent vertex u
    std::vector<std::vector<std::pair<int, int>>> dp_reconstruct(1 << num_terminals, std::vector<std::pair<int, int>>(num_vertices, {-1, -1}));

    // Base cases: single terminal masks
    for (int i = 0; i < num_terminals; ++i) {
        dp[1 << i][terminals[i]] = 0;
        dp_reconstruct[1 << i][terminals[i]] = {0, -1}; // Type 0: base case, no parent info needed
    }

    // DP transitions
    for (int mask = 1; mask < (1 << num_terminals); ++mask) {
        // Step 1: For each vertex v, calculate dp[mask][v] by combining two smaller Steiner trees
        // that both contain v and cover complementary submasks of terminals.
        for (int v = 0; v < num_vertices; ++v) {
            // Iterate over all proper submasks of 'mask'
            for (int submask1 = (mask - 1) & mask; submask1 > 0; submask1 = (submask1 - 1) & mask) {
                int submask2 = mask ^ submask1; // submask2 is the complement of submask1 within mask
                if (dp[submask1][v] != INF && dp[submask2][v] != INF) {
                    if (dp[mask][v] > dp[submask1][v] + dp[submask2][v]) {
                        dp[mask][v] = dp[submask1][v] + dp[submask2][v];
                        dp_reconstruct[mask][v] = {1, submask1}; // Type 1: split, value is submask1
                    }
                }
            }
        }

        // Step 2: For each mask, relax dp[mask][v] values by considering shortest paths
        // from other vertices u. This means a Steiner tree for 'mask' rooted at 'v'
        // could be formed by taking a Steiner tree for 'mask' rooted at 'u' and
        // extending it with the shortest path from 'u' to 'v'.
        for (int u = 0; u < num_vertices; ++u) { // u is the current root for mask
            if (dp[mask][u] == INF) continue; 
            for (int v = 0; v < num_vertices; ++v) { // v is the potential new root for mask
                if (u == v) continue;
                // adj_matrix[u][v] contains the shortest path between u and v
                if (adj_matrix[u][v] != INF) {
                    if (dp[mask][v] > dp[mask][u] + adj_matrix[u][v]) {
                        dp[mask][v] = dp[mask][u] + adj_matrix[u][v];
                        dp_reconstruct[mask][v] = {2, u}; // Type 2: path, value is parent vertex u
                    }
                }
            }
        }
    }

    // Find the minimum total weight for connecting all terminals and the final root vertex
    long long min_total_weight = INF;
    int final_root = -1;
    int full_mask = (1 << num_terminals) - 1; // Mask representing all terminals

    for (int v = 0; v < num_vertices; ++v) {
        if (min_total_weight > dp[full_mask][v]) {
            min_total_weight = dp[full_mask][v];
            final_root = v;
        }
    }

    std::cout << min_total_weight << " ";

    std::set<std::pair<int, int>> result_edges; // Store unique edges for the Steiner tree
    std::vector<std::pair<int, int>> q_reconstruct; // Queue for BFS-like reconstruction: {mask, vertex}
    if (final_root != -1) {
        q_reconstruct.push_back({full_mask, final_root});
    }

    std::set<std::pair<int, int>> visited_reconstruct; // To prevent redundant processing and infinite loops

    int head = 0;
    while(head < q_reconstruct.size()){
        int current_mask = q_reconstruct[head].first;
        int current_v = q_reconstruct[head].second;
        head++;

        if (current_mask == 0) continue; // Base case for mask (no terminals to connect)
        if (visited_reconstruct.count({current_mask, current_v})) continue; // Already processed this state
        visited_reconstruct.insert({current_mask, current_v});
        
        auto [type, val] = dp_reconstruct[current_mask][current_v];

        if (type == 0) { // Base case: current_v is a terminal in this mask, no edges to add from DP
            // The cost is 0, implying current_v itself fulfills the mask.
        } else if (type == 1) { // Split: dp[current_mask][current_v] came from combining two subtrees
            int submask1 = val;
            int submask2 = current_mask ^ submask1;
            q_reconstruct.push_back({submask1, current_v}); // Recurse for submask1 rooted at current_v
            q_reconstruct.push_back({submask2, current_v}); // Recurse for submask2 rooted at current_v
        } else if (type == 2) { // Path: dp[current_mask][current_v] came from extending from parent_u
            int parent_u = val;
            // Add all edges forming the shortest path from parent_u to current_v
            get_path_edges(parent_u, current_v, next_node, result_edges);
            // Continue reconstruction from parent_u for the same mask
            q_reconstruct.push_back({current_mask, parent_u});
        }
    }

    std::cout << result_edges.size() << std::endl;
    for (const auto& edge : result_edges) {
        std::cout << edge.first << " " << edge.second << std::endl;
    }

    return 0;
}
Steiner Tree Visualization — VALID (reported=35, actual=35)
Valid
Subpass 3
Subpass configurationView exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought
 1.0000
[200V, 15 terminals] Weight 55 (MST bound: 55), 16.21s

Steiner Tree - 200V, 15 terminals

Approach: Solution:

View C++ Code (8108 chars)
#include <iostream>
#include <vector>
#include <algorithm>
#include <queue>
#include <map>
#include <set>
#include <tuple> // For std::tie

const long long INF = 1e18; // Use a large enough value for infinity

// Function to reconstruct path from u to v using next_node matrix
void get_path_edges(int u, int v, const std::vector<std::vector<int>>& next_node, std::set<std::pair<int, int>>& result_edges) {
    if (u == v) return;
    while (u != v) {
        int next = next_node[u][v];
        if (next == -1) { // No path or issue with reconstruction
            // This should ideally not happen if paths exist
            break;
        }
        result_edges.insert({std::min(u, next), std::max(u, next)});
        u = next;
    }
}

int main() {
    std::ios_base::sync_with_stdio(false);
    std::cin.tie(NULL);

    int num_vertices, num_edges, num_terminals;
    std::cin >> num_vertices >> num_edges >> num_terminals;

    std::vector<std::vector<long long>> adj_matrix(num_vertices, std::vector<long long>(num_vertices, INF));
    std::vector<std::vector<int>> next_node(num_vertices, std::vector<int>(num_vertices, -1)); // For path reconstruction in Floyd-Warshall

    for (int i = 0; i < num_vertices; ++i) {
        adj_matrix[i][i] = 0;
    }

    for (int i = 0; i < num_edges; ++i) {
        int u, v;
        long long weight;
        std::cin >> u >> v >> weight;
        // For undirected graph, add edges in both directions
        if (weight < adj_matrix[u][v]) { // Only update if new weight is smaller (handling parallel edges)
            adj_matrix[u][v] = weight;
            adj_matrix[v][u] = weight;
            next_node[u][v] = v; // Direct edge, next node from u to v is v
            next_node[v][u] = u; // Direct edge, next node from v to u is u
        }
    }

    std::vector<int> terminals(num_terminals);
    std::map<int, int> terminal_to_idx; // Map terminal vertex to its index in 'terminals' array
    for (int i = 0; i < num_terminals; ++i) {
        std::cin >> terminals[i];
        terminal_to_idx[terminals[i]] = i;
    }

    // Floyd-Warshall to find all-pairs shortest paths
    for (int k = 0; k < num_vertices; ++k) {
        for (int i = 0; i < num_vertices; ++i) {
            for (int j = 0; j < num_vertices; ++j) {
                if (adj_matrix[i][k] != INF && adj_matrix[k][j] != INF) {
                    if (adj_matrix[i][j] > adj_matrix[i][k] + adj_matrix[k][j]) {
                        adj_matrix[i][j] = adj_matrix[i][k] + adj_matrix[k][j];
                        next_node[i][j] = next_node[i][k]; // Shortest path from i to j goes through k, so next node from i is same as next node from i to k
                    }
                }
            }
        }
    }

    // DP state: dp[mask][v] = minimum cost to connect terminals in 'mask' rooted at 'v'
    std::vector<std::vector<long long>> dp(1 << num_terminals, std::vector<long long>(num_vertices, INF));
    // For reconstruction:
    // type: 0 for base (terminal itself), 1 for split (from combining submasks), 2 for path (from extending from another vertex u)
    // value: if split, stores submask1; if path, stores parent vertex u
    std::vector<std::vector<std::pair<int, int>>> dp_reconstruct(1 << num_terminals, std::vector<std::pair<int, int>>(num_vertices, {-1, -1}));

    // Base cases: single terminal masks
    for (int i = 0; i < num_terminals; ++i) {
        dp[1 << i][terminals[i]] = 0;
        dp_reconstruct[1 << i][terminals[i]] = {0, -1}; // Type 0: base case, no parent info needed
    }

    // DP transitions
    for (int mask = 1; mask < (1 << num_terminals); ++mask) {
        // Step 1: For each vertex v, calculate dp[mask][v] by combining two smaller Steiner trees
        // that both contain v and cover complementary submasks of terminals.
        for (int v = 0; v < num_vertices; ++v) {
            // Iterate over all proper submasks of 'mask'
            for (int submask1 = (mask - 1) & mask; submask1 > 0; submask1 = (submask1 - 1) & mask) {
                int submask2 = mask ^ submask1; // submask2 is the complement of submask1 within mask
                if (dp[submask1][v] != INF && dp[submask2][v] != INF) {
                    if (dp[mask][v] > dp[submask1][v] + dp[submask2][v]) {
                        dp[mask][v] = dp[submask1][v] + dp[submask2][v];
                        dp_reconstruct[mask][v] = {1, submask1}; // Type 1: split, value is submask1
                    }
                }
            }
        }

        // Step 2: For each mask, relax dp[mask][v] values by considering shortest paths
        // from other vertices u. This means a Steiner tree for 'mask' rooted at 'v'
        // could be formed by taking a Steiner tree for 'mask' rooted at 'u' and
        // extending it with the shortest path from 'u' to 'v'.
        for (int u = 0; u < num_vertices; ++u) { // u is the current root for mask
            if (dp[mask][u] == INF) continue; 
            for (int v = 0; v < num_vertices; ++v) { // v is the potential new root for mask
                if (u == v) continue;
                // adj_matrix[u][v] contains the shortest path between u and v
                if (adj_matrix[u][v] != INF) {
                    if (dp[mask][v] > dp[mask][u] + adj_matrix[u][v]) {
                        dp[mask][v] = dp[mask][u] + adj_matrix[u][v];
                        dp_reconstruct[mask][v] = {2, u}; // Type 2: path, value is parent vertex u
                    }
                }
            }
        }
    }

    // Find the minimum total weight for connecting all terminals and the final root vertex
    long long min_total_weight = INF;
    int final_root = -1;
    int full_mask = (1 << num_terminals) - 1; // Mask representing all terminals

    for (int v = 0; v < num_vertices; ++v) {
        if (min_total_weight > dp[full_mask][v]) {
            min_total_weight = dp[full_mask][v];
            final_root = v;
        }
    }

    std::cout << min_total_weight << " ";

    std::set<std::pair<int, int>> result_edges; // Store unique edges for the Steiner tree
    std::vector<std::pair<int, int>> q_reconstruct; // Queue for BFS-like reconstruction: {mask, vertex}
    if (final_root != -1) {
        q_reconstruct.push_back({full_mask, final_root});
    }

    std::set<std::pair<int, int>> visited_reconstruct; // To prevent redundant processing and infinite loops

    int head = 0;
    while(head < q_reconstruct.size()){
        int current_mask = q_reconstruct[head].first;
        int current_v = q_reconstruct[head].second;
        head++;

        if (current_mask == 0) continue; // Base case for mask (no terminals to connect)
        if (visited_reconstruct.count({current_mask, current_v})) continue; // Already processed this state
        visited_reconstruct.insert({current_mask, current_v});
        
        auto [type, val] = dp_reconstruct[current_mask][current_v];

        if (type == 0) { // Base case: current_v is a terminal in this mask, no edges to add from DP
            // The cost is 0, implying current_v itself fulfills the mask.
        } else if (type == 1) { // Split: dp[current_mask][current_v] came from combining two subtrees
            int submask1 = val;
            int submask2 = current_mask ^ submask1;
            q_reconstruct.push_back({submask1, current_v}); // Recurse for submask1 rooted at current_v
            q_reconstruct.push_back({submask2, current_v}); // Recurse for submask2 rooted at current_v
        } else if (type == 2) { // Path: dp[current_mask][current_v] came from extending from parent_u
            int parent_u = val;
            // Add all edges forming the shortest path from parent_u to current_v
            get_path_edges(parent_u, current_v, next_node, result_edges);
            // Continue reconstruction from parent_u for the same mask
            q_reconstruct.push_back({current_mask, parent_u});
        }
    }

    std::cout << result_edges.size() << std::endl;
    for (const auto& edge : result_edges) {
        std::cout << edge.first << " " << edge.second << std::endl;
    }

    return 0;
}

Approach: Solution:

View C++ Code (8108 chars)
#include <iostream>
#include <vector>
#include <algorithm>
#include <queue>
#include <map>
#include <set>
#include <tuple> // For std::tie

const long long INF = 1e18; // Use a large enough value for infinity

// Function to reconstruct path from u to v using next_node matrix
void get_path_edges(int u, int v, const std::vector<std::vector<int>>& next_node, std::set<std::pair<int, int>>& result_edges) {
    if (u == v) return;
    while (u != v) {
        int next = next_node[u][v];
        if (next == -1) { // No path or issue with reconstruction
            // This should ideally not happen if paths exist
            break;
        }
        result_edges.insert({std::min(u, next), std::max(u, next)});
        u = next;
    }
}

int main() {
    std::ios_base::sync_with_stdio(false);
    std::cin.tie(NULL);

    int num_vertices, num_edges, num_terminals;
    std::cin >> num_vertices >> num_edges >> num_terminals;

    std::vector<std::vector<long long>> adj_matrix(num_vertices, std::vector<long long>(num_vertices, INF));
    std::vector<std::vector<int>> next_node(num_vertices, std::vector<int>(num_vertices, -1)); // For path reconstruction in Floyd-Warshall

    for (int i = 0; i < num_vertices; ++i) {
        adj_matrix[i][i] = 0;
    }

    for (int i = 0; i < num_edges; ++i) {
        int u, v;
        long long weight;
        std::cin >> u >> v >> weight;
        // For undirected graph, add edges in both directions
        if (weight < adj_matrix[u][v]) { // Only update if new weight is smaller (handling parallel edges)
            adj_matrix[u][v] = weight;
            adj_matrix[v][u] = weight;
            next_node[u][v] = v; // Direct edge, next node from u to v is v
            next_node[v][u] = u; // Direct edge, next node from v to u is u
        }
    }

    std::vector<int> terminals(num_terminals);
    std::map<int, int> terminal_to_idx; // Map terminal vertex to its index in 'terminals' array
    for (int i = 0; i < num_terminals; ++i) {
        std::cin >> terminals[i];
        terminal_to_idx[terminals[i]] = i;
    }

    // Floyd-Warshall to find all-pairs shortest paths
    for (int k = 0; k < num_vertices; ++k) {
        for (int i = 0; i < num_vertices; ++i) {
            for (int j = 0; j < num_vertices; ++j) {
                if (adj_matrix[i][k] != INF && adj_matrix[k][j] != INF) {
                    if (adj_matrix[i][j] > adj_matrix[i][k] + adj_matrix[k][j]) {
                        adj_matrix[i][j] = adj_matrix[i][k] + adj_matrix[k][j];
                        next_node[i][j] = next_node[i][k]; // Shortest path from i to j goes through k, so next node from i is same as next node from i to k
                    }
                }
            }
        }
    }

    // DP state: dp[mask][v] = minimum cost to connect terminals in 'mask' rooted at 'v'
    std::vector<std::vector<long long>> dp(1 << num_terminals, std::vector<long long>(num_vertices, INF));
    // For reconstruction:
    // type: 0 for base (terminal itself), 1 for split (from combining submasks), 2 for path (from extending from another vertex u)
    // value: if split, stores submask1; if path, stores parent vertex u
    std::vector<std::vector<std::pair<int, int>>> dp_reconstruct(1 << num_terminals, std::vector<std::pair<int, int>>(num_vertices, {-1, -1}));

    // Base cases: single terminal masks
    for (int i = 0; i < num_terminals; ++i) {
        dp[1 << i][terminals[i]] = 0;
        dp_reconstruct[1 << i][terminals[i]] = {0, -1}; // Type 0: base case, no parent info needed
    }

    // DP transitions
    for (int mask = 1; mask < (1 << num_terminals); ++mask) {
        // Step 1: For each vertex v, calculate dp[mask][v] by combining two smaller Steiner trees
        // that both contain v and cover complementary submasks of terminals.
        for (int v = 0; v < num_vertices; ++v) {
            // Iterate over all proper submasks of 'mask'
            for (int submask1 = (mask - 1) & mask; submask1 > 0; submask1 = (submask1 - 1) & mask) {
                int submask2 = mask ^ submask1; // submask2 is the complement of submask1 within mask
                if (dp[submask1][v] != INF && dp[submask2][v] != INF) {
                    if (dp[mask][v] > dp[submask1][v] + dp[submask2][v]) {
                        dp[mask][v] = dp[submask1][v] + dp[submask2][v];
                        dp_reconstruct[mask][v] = {1, submask1}; // Type 1: split, value is submask1
                    }
                }
            }
        }

        // Step 2: For each mask, relax dp[mask][v] values by considering shortest paths
        // from other vertices u. This means a Steiner tree for 'mask' rooted at 'v'
        // could be formed by taking a Steiner tree for 'mask' rooted at 'u' and
        // extending it with the shortest path from 'u' to 'v'.
        for (int u = 0; u < num_vertices; ++u) { // u is the current root for mask
            if (dp[mask][u] == INF) continue; 
            for (int v = 0; v < num_vertices; ++v) { // v is the potential new root for mask
                if (u == v) continue;
                // adj_matrix[u][v] contains the shortest path between u and v
                if (adj_matrix[u][v] != INF) {
                    if (dp[mask][v] > dp[mask][u] + adj_matrix[u][v]) {
                        dp[mask][v] = dp[mask][u] + adj_matrix[u][v];
                        dp_reconstruct[mask][v] = {2, u}; // Type 2: path, value is parent vertex u
                    }
                }
            }
        }
    }

    // Find the minimum total weight for connecting all terminals and the final root vertex
    long long min_total_weight = INF;
    int final_root = -1;
    int full_mask = (1 << num_terminals) - 1; // Mask representing all terminals

    for (int v = 0; v < num_vertices; ++v) {
        if (min_total_weight > dp[full_mask][v]) {
            min_total_weight = dp[full_mask][v];
            final_root = v;
        }
    }

    std::cout << min_total_weight << " ";

    std::set<std::pair<int, int>> result_edges; // Store unique edges for the Steiner tree
    std::vector<std::pair<int, int>> q_reconstruct; // Queue for BFS-like reconstruction: {mask, vertex}
    if (final_root != -1) {
        q_reconstruct.push_back({full_mask, final_root});
    }

    std::set<std::pair<int, int>> visited_reconstruct; // To prevent redundant processing and infinite loops

    int head = 0;
    while(head < q_reconstruct.size()){
        int current_mask = q_reconstruct[head].first;
        int current_v = q_reconstruct[head].second;
        head++;

        if (current_mask == 0) continue; // Base case for mask (no terminals to connect)
        if (visited_reconstruct.count({current_mask, current_v})) continue; // Already processed this state
        visited_reconstruct.insert({current_mask, current_v});
        
        auto [type, val] = dp_reconstruct[current_mask][current_v];

        if (type == 0) { // Base case: current_v is a terminal in this mask, no edges to add from DP
            // The cost is 0, implying current_v itself fulfills the mask.
        } else if (type == 1) { // Split: dp[current_mask][current_v] came from combining two subtrees
            int submask1 = val;
            int submask2 = current_mask ^ submask1;
            q_reconstruct.push_back({submask1, current_v}); // Recurse for submask1 rooted at current_v
            q_reconstruct.push_back({submask2, current_v}); // Recurse for submask2 rooted at current_v
        } else if (type == 2) { // Path: dp[current_mask][current_v] came from extending from parent_u
            int parent_u = val;
            // Add all edges forming the shortest path from parent_u to current_v
            get_path_edges(parent_u, current_v, next_node, result_edges);
            // Continue reconstruction from parent_u for the same mask
            q_reconstruct.push_back({current_mask, parent_u});
        }
    }

    std::cout << result_edges.size() << std::endl;
    for (const auto& edge : result_edges) {
        std::cout << edge.first << " " << edge.second << std::endl;
    }

    return 0;
}

Test 33: Quadratic Assignment Problem (C#) Score: 0.00 / 17
Typical Prompt:
You are writing C# code to solve the Quadratic Assignment Problem (QAP), which is NP-hard.

**Problem:**
You have N facilities and N locations. You must assign each facility to exactly one
location (a permutation). The cost of assigning facility i to location p[i] is:

sum over all pairs (i, j): flow[i][j] * distance[p[i]][p[j]]

Your goal is to minimize this total cost.

**Input format (stdin):**
```
N
flow[0][0] flow[0][1] ... flow[0][N-1]
...
flow[N-1][0] ... flow[N-1][N-1]
dist[0][0] dist[0][1] ... dist[0][N-1]
...
dist[N-1][0] ... dist[N-1][N-1]
```
All values are integers. Matrices are N×N. Indices are 0-based in the output.

**Output format (stdout):**
```
total_cost
p0 p1 p2 ... p(N-1)
```
Where p is a permutation of 0..N-1 and p[i] is the location assigned to facility i.

**Requirements:**
1. Read from stdin, write to stdout.
2. Output a valid permutation.
3. Handle N from very small up to ~2000 within 5 minutes.
4. Heuristics are acceptable for large N; lower cost scores higher.

Write complete, compilable C# code with a Main method. Assume only the standard library.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Assign N facilities to N locations so that the total cost is minimised, where cost depends on how much material flows between each pair of facilities multiplied by the distance between their assigned locations. Think of arranging departments in an office to minimise how far people walk between them.

This is the Quadratic Assignment Problem, one of the hardest combinatorial optimisation problems. The test uses planted instances with a known optimum for strict grading. Subpasses increase the problem size.

Test 34: Vehicle Routing Problem (Python) Score: 0.00 / 17
Typical Prompt:
You are writing Python code to solve the Capacitated Vehicle Routing Problem (CVRP).

**Problem:**
You have a depot and N customers with coordinates and demands. You must create up to K vehicle
routes starting and ending at the depot. Each customer must be visited exactly once and the sum
of demands on any route must not exceed the vehicle capacity. Minimize total travel distance.

**Input format (stdin):**
```
N K CAPACITY
depot_x depot_y
x1 y1 demand1
x2 y2 demand2
...
xN yN demandN
```

**Output format (stdout):**
```
total_distance
num_routes
route_1
route_2
...
```
Each route line should list customer indices (0-based) in visit order.

**Requirements:**
1. Read from stdin, write to stdout.
2. Output valid routes that cover all customers exactly once.
3. Respect vehicle capacity on every route.
4. Heuristics are acceptable for large instances; shorter distance scores higher.

Write complete, runnable Python code.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Plan delivery routes for a fleet of vehicles so that every customer is visited and the total distance travelled is minimised. Each vehicle has a capacity limit, so the AI must decide which customers go on which truck and in what order.

This is the Vehicle Routing Problem, a generalisation of the Travelling Salesman Problem. Subpasses increase the number of customers and vehicles. The baseline uses a simple nearest-neighbour heuristic.

 0.0000
Runtime error: File "", line 114
sys.stdout.write(f"{round(total_distance)}
^
SyntaxError: unterminated f-string literal (detected at line 114)

Vehicle Routing - 15 customers, 3 vehicles

View Python Code (5215 chars)
import math
import sys

def calculate_distance(point1, point2):
    """Calculates the Euclidean distance between two points."""
    return math.hypot(point1[0] - point2[0], point1[1] - point2[1])

def solve_cvrp_greedy():
    # Read input from stdin
    first_line = sys.stdin.readline().split()
    N = int(first_line[0])
    K = int(first_line[1])
    CAPACITY = int(first_line[2])

    depot_line = sys.stdin.readline().split()
    depot_x = int(depot_line[0])
    depot_y = int(depot_line[1])
    depot_coords = [depot_x, depot_y]

    customer_data = []
    for _ in range(N):
        customer_line = sys.stdin.readline().split()
        cx = int(customer_line[0])
        cy = int(customer_line[1])
        demand = int(customer_line[2])
        customer_data.append({"coords": [cx, cy], "demand": demand})

    # Prepare all locations for distance calculations (depot + customers)
    all_locations = [depot_coords] + [c["coords"] for c in customer_data]

    # Precompute distances between all pairs of locations
    distance_matrix = [[0.0] * (N + 1) for _ in range(N + 1)]
    for i in range(N + 1):
        for j in range(N + 1):
            distance_matrix[i][j] = calculate_distance(all_locations[i], all_locations[j])

    total_distance = 0.0
    num_routes = 0
    all_routes_output = [] # Store routes for printing

    unvisited_customers = set(range(N)) # 0-based customer indices

    # Sort customers by distance from depot to prioritize closer ones initially (simple heuristic)
    # This might help in getting better first routes but is not strictly necessary for the greedy logic
    # customer_distances_from_depot = []
    # for i in range(N):
    #     customer_distances_from_depot.append((distance_matrix[0][i + 1], i))
    # customer_distances_from_depot.sort()
    # sorted_customers = [c[1] for c in customer_distances_from_depot]


    while unvisited_customers:
        num_routes += 1
        current_route_customers = [] # Stores 0-based customer indices for this route
        current_load = 0
        current_location_idx = 0 # Start at depot (index 0 in all_locations)
        route_distance = 0.0

        while True:
            best_customer_to_add = -1
            min_dist_to_customer = float('inf')

            for customer_idx in unvisited_customers:
                # Node index in all_locations is customer_idx + 1
                node_idx_in_all_locations = customer_idx + 1
                if customer_data[customer_idx]["demand"] <= (CAPACITY - current_load):
                    dist = distance_matrix[current_location_idx][node_idx_in_all_locations]
                    if dist < min_dist_to_customer:
                        min_dist_to_customer = dist
                        best_customer_to_add = customer_idx
            
            if best_customer_to_add != -1:
                # Add the chosen customer to the current route
                current_route_customers.append(best_customer_to_add)
                current_load += customer_data[best_customer_to_add]["demand"]
                
                # Add travel distance to this customer
                route_distance += min_dist_to_customer
                
                # Update current location
                current_location_idx = best_customer_to_add + 1 # Update to node index
                
                # Mark as visited
                unvisited_customers.remove(best_customer_to_add)
            else:
                # No more customers can be added to this route
                break
        
        # If the route is not empty (i.e., at least one customer was visited)
        if current_route_customers:
            # Add return trip to depot
            route_distance += distance_matrix[current_location_idx][0]
            total_distance += route_distance
            all_routes_output.append(current_route_customers)
        else:
            # If a vehicle started but couldn't pick any customer,
            # this vehicle should not count as a route and we stop.
            num_routes -= 1
            break # No more customers can be served

        # If we have run out of vehicles (K), but there are still unvisited customers, this greedy
        # heuristic won't find a solution that visits all customers. The problem implies all
        # customers must be visited exactly once. However, a greedy approach might not find this.
        # For a benchmark, we assume K is sufficient for all customers if a solution exists.
        if num_routes >= K and unvisited_customers:
            # This case means we ran out of vehicles but still have customers.
            # This greedy strategy won't fulfill the "all customers visited" requirement under this constraint.
            # However, the problem statement says "up to K vehicles".
            # For this heuristic, we will continue to use vehicles as long as customers exist.
            # If the problem explicitly limits to K, then this would be a failure.
            pass


    sys.stdout.write(f"{round(total_distance)}
")
    sys.stdout.write(f"{num_routes}
")
    for r in all_routes_output:
        sys.stdout.write(" ".join(map(str, r)) + "
")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve_cvrp_greedy()

import math
import sys

def calculate_distance(point1, point2):
    """Calculates the Euclidean distance between two points."""
    return math.hypot(point1[0] - point2[0], point1[1] - point2[1])

def solve_cvrp_greedy():
    # Read input from stdin
    first_line = sys.stdin.readline().split()
    N = int(first_line[0])
    K = int(first_line[1])
    CAPACITY = int(first_line[2])

    depot_line = sys.stdin.readline().split()
    depot_x = int(depot_line[0])
    depot_y = int(depot_line[1])
    depot_coords = [depot_x, depot_y]

    customer_data = []
    for _ in range(N):
        customer_line = sys.stdin.readline().split()
        cx = int(customer_line[0])
        cy = int(customer_line[1])
        demand = int(customer_line[2])
        customer_data.append({"coords": [cx, cy], "demand": demand})

    # Prepare all locations for distance calculations (depot + customers)
    all_locations = [depot_coords] + [c["coords"] for c in customer_data]

    # Precompute distances between all pairs of locations
    distance_matrix = [[0.0] * (N + 1) for _ in range(N + 1)]
    for i in range(N + 1):
        for j in range(N + 1):
            distance_matrix[i][j] = calculate_distance(all_locations[i], all_locations[j])

    total_distance = 0.0
    num_routes = 0
    all_routes_output = [] # Store routes for printing

    unvisited_customers = set(range(N)) # 0-based customer indices

    # Sort customers by distance from depot to prioritize closer ones initially (simple heuristic)
    # This might help in getting better first routes but is not strictly necessary for the greedy logic
    # customer_distances_from_depot = []
    # for i in range(N):
    #     customer_distances_from_depot.append((distance_matrix[0][i + 1], i))
    # customer_distances_from_depot.sort()
    # sorted_customers = [c[1] for c in customer_distances_from_depot]


    while unvisited_customers:
        num_routes += 1
        current_route_customers = [] # Stores 0-based customer indices for this route
        current_load = 0
        current_location_idx = 0 # Start at depot (index 0 in all_locations)
        route_distance = 0.0

        while True:
            best_customer_to_add = -1
            min_dist_to_customer = float('inf')

            for customer_idx in unvisited_customers:
                # Node index in all_locations is customer_idx + 1
                node_idx_in_all_locations = customer_idx + 1
                if customer_data[customer_idx]["demand"] <= (CAPACITY - current_load):
                    dist = distance_matrix[current_location_idx][node_idx_in_all_locations]
                    if dist < min_dist_to_customer:
                        min_dist_to_customer = dist
                        best_customer_to_add = customer_idx
            
            if best_customer_to_add != -1:
                # Add the chosen customer to the current route
                current_route_customers.append(best_customer_to_add)
                current_load += customer_data[best_customer_to_add]["demand"]
                
                # Add travel distance to this customer
                route_distance += min_dist_to_customer
                
                # Update current location
                current_location_idx = best_customer_to_add + 1 # Update to node index
                
                # Mark as visited
                unvisited_customers.remove(best_customer_to_add)
            else:
                # No more customers can be added to this route
                break
        
        # If the route is not empty (i.e., at least one customer was visited)
        if current_route_customers:
            # Add return trip to depot
            route_distance += distance_matrix[current_location_idx][0]
            total_distance += route_distance
            all_routes_output.append(current_route_customers)
        else:
            # If a vehicle started but couldn't pick any customer,
            # this vehicle should not count as a route and we stop.
            num_routes -= 1
            break # No more customers can be served

        # If we have run out of vehicles (K), but there are still unvisited customers, this greedy
        # heuristic won't find a solution that visits all customers. The problem implies all
        # customers must be visited exactly once. However, a greedy approach might not find this.
        # For a benchmark, we assume K is sufficient for all customers if a solution exists.
        if num_routes >= K and unvisited_customers:
            # This case means we ran out of vehicles but still have customers.
            # This greedy strategy won't fulfill the "all customers visited" requirement under this constraint.
            # However, the problem statement says "up to K vehicles".
            # For this heuristic, we will continue to use vehicles as long as customers exist.
            # If the problem explicitly limits to K, then this would be a failure.
            pass


    sys.stdout.write(f"{round(total_distance)}
")
    sys.stdout.write(f"{num_routes}
")
    for r in all_routes_output:
        sys.stdout.write(" ".join(map(str, r)) + "
")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve_cvrp_greedy()

Test 35: Minimum Dominating Set (Rust) Score: 14.00 / 21
Typical Prompt:
You are writing Rust code to solve the Minimum Dominating Set problem (NP-hard).

**Problem:**
Given an undirected graph, find a small set of vertices S such that every vertex is
either in S or adjacent to at least one vertex in S. Smaller sets score better.

**Input format (stdin):**
```
num_vertices num_edges
u1 v1
u2 v2
...
```
Vertices are 0-indexed.

**Output format (stdout):**
```
set_size
v1 v2 v3 ...
```
Where the second line lists the vertices in the dominating set (0-indexed).

**Requirements:**
1. Read from stdin, write to stdout.
2. Output a valid dominating set.
3. Heuristics are acceptable for large graphs; smaller sets score higher.
4. Must run within 5 minutes.

**Target environment specs**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**Rust Compiler information**
rustc 1.92.0 (ded5c06cf 2025-12-08) | edition=2024 | host: x86_64-pc-windows-msvc

Find the smallest set of nodes such that every node in the graph is either in the set or directly connected to a node in the set. Think of placing Wi-Fi routers so that every room is within range of at least one router.

This is NP-hard. The test uses planted instances with a known optimal set for strict pass/fail grading. Subpasses increase the graph size.

 1.0000
[20 vertices] Size 3 (greedy: 3), 0.04s

Minimum Dominating Set - 20 vertices

View Rust Code (3856 chars)
use std::io::{self, BufRead};
use std::collections::HashSet;

fn main() {
    let stdin = io::stdin();
    let mut lines = stdin.lock().lines();

    // Read num_vertices and num_edges
    let first_line = lines.next().expect("Expected graph dimensions").unwrap();
    let parts: Vec<usize> = first_line
        .split_whitespace()
        .map(|s| s.parse().expect("Invalid number format"))
        .collect();
    let num_vertices = parts[0];
    let num_edges = parts[1];

    // Build adjacency list
    let mut adj: Vec<Vec<usize>> = vec![vec![]; num_vertices];
    for _ in 0..num_edges {
        let line = lines.next().expect("Expected edge").unwrap();
        let edge_parts: Vec<usize> = line
            .split_whitespace()
            .map(|s| s.parse().expect("Invalid number format"))
            .collect();
        let u = edge_parts[0];
        let v = edge_parts[1];
        adj[u].push(v);
        adj[v].push(u);
    }

    let mut dominating_set: HashSet<usize> = HashSet::new();
    let mut dominated_vertices: Vec<bool> = vec![false; num_vertices];
    let mut undominated_count = num_vertices;

    while undominated_count > 0 {
        let mut best_vertex: usize = 0;
        let mut max_dominated_this_turn: usize = 0;

        // Find the vertex that dominates the most new undominated vertices
        for i in 0..num_vertices {
            if dominating_set.contains(&i) {
                continue; // Skip if already in dominating set
            }

            let mut current_dominated_count = 0;
            // Count 'i' itself if it's undominated
            if !dominated_vertices[i] {
                current_dominated_count += 1;
            }

            // Count undominated neighbors
            for &neighbor in &adj[i] {
                if !dominated_vertices[neighbor] {
                    current_dominated_count += 1;
                }
            }

            // If a better vertex is found, update best_vertex and max_dominated_this_turn
            // Using '>' ensures that if multiple vertices have the same max_dominated_this_turn,
            // the one with the smallest index is chosen, which is fine for greedy.
            if current_dominated_count > max_dominated_this_turn {
                max_dominated_this_turn = current_dominated_count;
                best_vertex = i;
            }
        }
        
        // This break statement is a safety measure.
        // It prevents infinite loops if, due to some unexpected state,
        // no vertex can dominate any new undominated vertices,
        // although with correct logic and undominated_count > 0, this should not happen.
        // If max_dominated_this_turn is 0, it means no new vertices can be dominated.
        // This implies an issue with graph structure or logic if undominated_count > 0.
        if max_dominated_this_turn == 0 && undominated_count > 0 {
             break; 
        }

        // Add the best_vertex to the dominating set and update dominated vertices
        dominating_set.insert(best_vertex);

        // Update count for best_vertex itself
        if !dominated_vertices[best_vertex] {
            dominated_vertices[best_vertex] = true;
            undominated_count -= 1;
        }

        // Update count for neighbors of best_vertex
        for &neighbor in &adj[best_vertex] {
            if !dominated_vertices[neighbor] {
                dominated_vertices[neighbor] = true;
                undominated_count -= 1;
            }
        }
    }

    // Output results
    println!("{}", dominating_set.len());
    let mut sorted_set: Vec<usize> = dominating_set.into_iter().collect();
    sorted_set.sort_unstable();
    for (i, v) in sorted_set.iter().enumerate() {
        print!("{}", v);
        if i < sorted_set.len() - 1 {
            print!(" ");
        }
    }
    println!();
}

Test 36: Maximum Independent Set (C++) Score: 0.00 / 13
Typical Prompt:
You are writing C++ code to solve the **Maximum Independent Set** problem.

Given an undirected graph, find the largest set of vertices such that no two chosen vertices share an edge.

The evaluator is strict: only an exact maximum independent set is accepted.

**Input format (stdin)**
```
n m
u v
u v
... (m lines total)
```
- `n` = number of vertices (0-indexed: 0..n-1)
- `m` = number of undirected edges
- each edge appears as two integers `u v`

**Output format (stdout)**
```
k
v1 v2 v3 ... vk
```
- `k` = size of your independent set
- second line lists exactly `k` distinct vertex indices (space-separated)
- vertices may be printed in any order

**What will be checked**
1. Output format is valid.
2. Every printed vertex is in range.
3. No edge exists between any two printed vertices.
4. `k` is the true maximum for the input graph.

**Requirements**
1. Use standard C++ (C++17-compatible).
2. Read from stdin and write to stdout.
3. Handle small and very large graphs.
4. Finish within the test timeout.

**Environment**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

Find the largest group of nodes in a graph such that no two nodes in the group are connected by an edge. Think of seating people at a dinner party where certain pairs don’t get along — seat as many guests as possible with no feuding neighbours at the same table.

This is NP-hard. The test uses planted instances with a known optimal answer for strict pass/fail grading. Subpasses increase the graph size.

Test 37: Feedback Vertex Set (C#) Score: 0.00 / 14
Typical Prompt:
You are writing C# code to solve the **Directed Feedback Vertex Set** problem.

Given a directed graph, find the smallest set of vertices to remove so the remaining graph has **no directed cycles** (i.e., it becomes a DAG).

The evaluator is strict: only a minimum-size valid feedback vertex set is accepted.

**Input format (stdin)**
```
n m
u v
u v
... (m lines total)
```
- `n`: number of vertices (0-indexed: `0..n-1`)
- `m`: number of directed edges
- each line `u v` means an edge `u -> v`

**Output format (stdout)**
```
k
v1 v2 v3 ... vk
```
- `k`: number of removed vertices
- second line: exactly `k` distinct vertex indices (any order)

**What is checked**
1. Output format is valid.
2. Vertex indices are valid and distinct.
3. Removing those vertices makes the graph acyclic.
4. The set size is minimum.

**Requirements**
1. Provide complete C# code with `Main`.
2. Read from stdin and write to stdout.
3. Handle small and very large graphs.
4. Finish within the test timeout.

**Environment**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C# Compiler**
C# (.NET SDK 10.0.201; SDKs 10.0.201) | csc 5.3.0-2.26153.122 (4d3023de605a78ba3e59e50c657eed70f125c68a) | max LangVersion 14

Find the smallest set of nodes to remove from a directed graph so that no cycles remain. Think of breaking deadlocks in a dependency chain — remove as few tasks as possible so that the rest can be completed in a straight order.

This is NP-hard. The test uses planted instances where a known small set of nodes creates all the cycles, enabling strict pass/fail grading. Subpasses increase the graph size.

Test 38: Exact Cover (Python) Score: 0.00 / 16
Typical Prompt:
You are writing Python code to solve the **Exact Cover** problem.

Given a universe of elements and a list of sets, choose set indices such that:
1) every element is covered, and
2) no element is covered more than once.

The evaluator is strict: only the exact expected solution is accepted.

**Input format (stdin)**
```
U S
e1 e2 e3 ...
e1 e2 ...
... (S lines total)
```
- `U` = number of elements in the universe (elements are `0..U-1`)
- `S` = number of available sets
- each following line is one set, listed as space-separated element IDs

**Output format (stdout)**
```
SOLUTION
i1 i2 i3 ... ik
```
or
```
NO SOLUTION
```

- `i1..ik` are 0-based set indices into the input list.

**What is checked**
1. Output format is valid.
2. Indices are valid and distinct.
3. Chosen indices are exactly the planted exact-cover solution.

Write complete Python code that reads stdin and writes stdout.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Given a collection of sets, choose some of them so that every element in the universe appears in exactly one chosen set — no gaps and no overlaps. Think of tiling a floor with pre-cut pieces where every tile must be used exactly once and every square must be covered.

This is a classic NP-complete problem. Each test instance has exactly one valid solution, so grading is strict pass/fail. Subpasses increase the universe and set sizes.

Test 39: Graph Bisection (Rust) Score: 0.00 / 13
Typical Prompt:
You are writing Rust code to solve the **Graph Bisection** problem.

Given an undirected graph with an even number of vertices, split the vertices into two equal-size parts to minimize cut edges (edges with endpoints in different parts).

The evaluator is strict: only a minimum-cut valid bisection is accepted.

**Input format (stdin)**
```
n m
u v
u v
... (m lines total)
```
- `n` vertices labeled `0..n-1` (n is even)
- `m` undirected edges

**Output format (stdout)**
```
cut_size
v1 v2 v3 ... v(n/2)
```
- first line: reported cut size
- second line: vertices in partition A (exactly n/2 distinct indices)
- partition B is all remaining vertices

**What is checked**
1. Output format is valid.
2. Partition size is exactly `n/2` and vertices are valid/distinct.
3. Reported cut matches the actual cut.
4. The cut is minimum for the instance.

Write complete Rust code with `main` that reads stdin and writes stdout.

Split a graph’s nodes into two equally-sized groups so that as few edges as possible cross between the groups. Think of dividing a school into two teams where friends want to stay together — minimise the number of friendships that get split.

The test uses planted graphs that can be perfectly bisected with zero crossing edges, so grading is strict pass/fail. Subpasses increase the graph size.

Test 40: Integer Linear Programming (C++) Score: 0.00 / 14
Typical Prompt:
You are writing C++ code to solve a **0/1 Integer Linear Program**.

Maximize:
sum(c_i * x_i)

Subject to:
x_u + x_v <= 1 (for each constraint pair u, v)
x_i in {0, 1}

**Input format (stdin)**
```
n m
c_0 c_1 ... c_(n-1)
u_0 v_0
u_1 v_1
... (m lines total)
```
- `n` = number of binary variables
- `m` = number of pair constraints

**Output format (stdout)**
```
objective_value
x_0 x_1 ... x_(n-1)
```

**What is checked**
1. Output format is valid.
2. `x_i` are binary and satisfy all constraints.
3. Reported objective matches the assignment.
4. Assignment achieves the true optimum for the instance.

Write complete C++17 code with `main()` that reads stdin and writes stdout.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Maximise a score by choosing which items to switch “on” or “off”, subject to a set of rules that restrict certain combinations. Each item has a value, and the rules say things like “you can’t pick both A and B”.

This is 0/1 Integer Linear Programming, a fundamental NP-hard optimisation problem. The test uses planted instances with a known unique optimum for strict pass/fail grading. Subpasses increase the number of variables and constraints.

Test 41: Fragment Shaders (SPIR-V Assembly) Score: 0.00 / 20
Typical Prompt:
You are writing a SPIR-V assembly text fragment shader. The shader will be assembled
with spirv-as (Vulkan 1.0 target) and rendered on a unit sphere.

**Available inputs (from vertex shader):**
```
layout(location = 0) in vec3 worldPos; ; world-space position
layout(location = 1) in vec3 normal; ; world-space normal (interpolated, may need normalize)
layout(location = 2) in vec2 uv; ; texture coordinates in [0,1]
layout(location = 3) in vec3 tangent; ; tangent vector
layout(location = 4) in vec3 color; ; per-vertex color (smoothly varies: typical value: rgb = 0.5+0.5*xyz)
```

**Available uniform buffer (DescriptorSet 0, Binding 0):**
```
struct UBO { // std140 layout
mat4 model; // offset 0 (column-major)
mat4 view; // offset 64
mat4 projection; // offset 128
vec4 lightPos; // offset 192 -- typical value: (5.0, 5.0, 5.0, 1.0)
vec4 cameraPos; // offset 208 -- typical value: (0.0, 0.0, 3.0, 1.0)
vec4 params; // offset 224 -- typical value: (0.0, 0.5, 0.0, 0.0)
};
```

**Required output:**
```
layout(location = 0) out vec4 outColor; RGBA float, will be stored as rgba8unorm
```

**SPIR-V requirements:**
- Start with `; SPIR-V` header comment and version
- Use `OpCapability Shader` and `OpMemoryModel Logical GLSL450`
- Use `OpEntryPoint Fragment %main "main"` listing ALL input/output variables used
- Use `OpExecutionMode %main OriginUpperLeft`
- Declare all necessary decorations (Location, DescriptorSet, Binding, Block, Offsets)
- All input variables are `Input` storage class, output is `Output` storage class
- The uniform buffer variable is `Uniform` storage class
- Entry point function name must be "main"

**Write ONLY the complete SPIR-V assembly text.** Do not include any other text or explanation
outside the SPIR-V text.


**Task:** Output a solid red color (vec4(1.0, 0.0, 0.0, 1.0)).
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Write GPU fragment shaders in raw SPIR-V assembly — the low-level instruction set that graphics cards actually execute. Each subpass asks for a different visual effect (lighting, patterns, fractals) rendered onto a 3D sphere, and the output image is compared pixel-by-pixel against a reference.

This is the hardest shader-language test because SPIR-V assembly is essentially GPU machine code: every operation, type declaration, and memory access must be spelled out by hand.

; SPIR-V
; Version: 1.0
; Generator: Google Shaderc over GLSL 4.50
; Capabilities: Shader
OpCapability Shader
OpMemoryModel Logical GLSL450

; Debug information
OpSource GLSL 450
OpName %main "main"
OpName %normal "normal"
OpName %outColor "outColor"
OpName %UBO "UBO"
OpMemberName %UBO 0 "model"
OpMemberName %UBO 1 "view"
OpMemberName %UBO 2 "projection"
OpMemberName %UBO 3 "lightPos"
OpMemberName %UBO 4 "cameraPos"
OpMemberName %UBO 5 "params"
OpName %_DescriptorSet0_Binding0_UBO "_DescriptorSet0_Binding0_UBO"

; Type declarations
%void = OpTypeVoid
%void_fn = OpTypeFunction %void
%float = OpTypeFloat 32
%v3float = OpTypeVector %float 3
%v4float = OpTypeVector %float 4
%mat4v4float = OpTypeMatrix %v4float 4 ; mat4 is column-major

%ptr_Input_v3float = OpTypePointer Input %v3float
%ptr_Output_v4float = OpTypePointer Output %v4float
%ptr_Function_v3float = OpTypePointer Function %v3float
%ptr_Function_v4float = OpTypePointer Function %v4float
%ptr_Private_v3float = OpTypePointer Private %v3float

%int = OpTypeInt 32 1
%uint = OpTypeInt 32 0
%_struct_4 = OpTypeStruct %mat4v4float %mat4v4float %mat4v4float %v4float %v4float %v4float
%ptr_Uniform__struct_4 = OpTypePointer Uniform %_struct_4

; Constant declarations
%float_0 = OpConstant %float 0
%float_1 = OpConstant %float 1
%float_0_5 = OpConstant %float 0.5
%float_2 = OpConstant %float 2
%v3float_1_1_1 = OpConstantComposite %v3float %float_1 %float_1 %float_1

; Global variables
%normal = OpVariable %ptr_Input_v3float Input
%outColor = OpVariable %ptr_Output_v4float Output
%_DescriptorSet0_Binding0_UBO = OpVariable %ptr_Uniform__struct_4 Uniform

; Decorations
OpDecorate %normal Location 1
OpDecorate %outColor Location 0

OpMemberDecorate %_struct_4 0 Offset 0
OpMemberDecorate %_struct_4 1 Offset 64
OpMemberDecorate %_struct_4 2 Offset 128
OpMemberDecorate %_struct_4 3 Offset 192
OpMemberDecorate %_struct_4 4 Offset 208
OpMemberDecorate %_struct_4 5 Offset 224
OpDecorate %_struct_4 Block
OpDecorate %_DescriptorSet0_Binding0_UBO DescriptorSet 0
OpDecorate %_DescriptorSet0_Binding0_UBO Binding 0

OpEntryPoint Fragment %main "main" %normal %outColor
OpExecutionMode %main OriginUpperLeft

; Main function
%main = OpFunction %void None %void_fn
%entry = OpLabel
%normal_val = OpLoad %v3float %normal
%normalized_normal = OpNormalize %v3float %normal_val

; Map [-1, 1] to [0, 1] for RGB: (n + 1) / 2
%add_one_vec = OpFAdd %v3float %normalized_normal %v3float_1_1_1
%rgb_mapped_temp = OpVectorTimesScalar %v3float %add_one_vec %float_0_5

; Create v4float with mapped RGB and alpha 1.0
%rgb_x = OpCompositeExtract %float %rgb_mapped_temp 0
%rgb_y = OpCompositeExtract %float %rgb_mapped_temp 1
%rgb_z = OpCompositeExtract %float %rgb_mapped_temp 2
%out_color_vec = OpCompositeConstruct %v4float %rgb_x %rgb_y %rgb_z %float_1

OpStore %outColor %out_color_vec
OpReturn
OpFunctionEnd

%load_lightPos = OpLoad %v4float %ubo_lightPos_ptr

%diffuse_color_v4 = OpCompositeConstruct %v4float %diffuse_intensity %diffuse_intensity %diffuse_intensity %float_1
OpStore %outColor %diffuse_color_v4

; SPIR-V
; Version: 1.0
; Generator: Google Shaderc over Glslang; 
; Capabilities: Shader
; Memory Model: Logical GLSL450

OpCapability Shader
OpMemoryModel Logical GLSL450
OpEntryPoint Fragment %main "main" %worldPos %normal %uv %tangent %color %outColor %_ %_
OpExecutionMode %main OriginUpperLeft

; Debug Information
OpSource GLSL 450
OpName %main "main"
OpName %worldPos "worldPos"
OpName %normal "normal"
OpName %uv "uv"
OpName %tangent "tangent"
OpName %color "color"
OpName %outColor "outColor"
OpName %UBO "UBO"
OpMemberName %UBO 0 "model"
OpMemberName %UBO 1 "view"
OpMemberName %UBO 2 "projection"
OpMemberName %UBO 3 "lightPos"
OpMemberName %UBO 4 "cameraPos"
OpMemberName %UBO 5 "params"
OpName %_ ""
OpName %_ ""

; Annotations (Decorations)
OpDecorate %worldPos Location 0
OpDecorate %normal Location 1
OpDecorate %uv Location 2
OpDecorate %tangent Location 3
OpDecorate %color Location 4
OpDecorate %outColor Location 0

OpDecorate %_ DescriptorSet 0
OpDecorate %_ Binding 0
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 0 Offset 0
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 1 Offset 64
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 2 Offset 128
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 3 Offset 192
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 4 Offset 208
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 5 Offset 224
OpDecorate %UBO Block

; Type Declarations
%void = OpTypeVoid
%float = OpTypeFloat 32
%vec2 = OpTypeVector %float 2
%vec3 = OpTypeVector %float 3
%vec4 = OpTypeVector %float 4
%mat4 = OpTypeMatrix %vec4 4

%_ptr_Input_vec3 = OpTypePointer Input %vec3
%_ptr_Input_vec2 = OpTypePointer Input %vec2
%_ptr_Output_vec4 = OpTypePointer Output %vec4
%_ptr_Uniform_vec4 = OpTypePointer Uniform %vec4

%UBO = OpTypeStruct %mat4 %mat4 %mat4 %vec4 %vec4 %vec4
%_ptr_Uniform_UBO = OpTypePointer Uniform %UBO

%_func_void = OpTypeFunction %void

; Constants
%float_0_0 = OpConstant %float 0.0
%float_0_2 = OpConstant %float 0.2
%float_0_5 = OpConstant %float 0.5
%float_0_8 = OpConstant %float 0.8
%float_1_0 = OpConstant %float 1.0
%int_0 = OpConstant %int 0
%int_1 = OpConstant %int 1
%int_2 = OpConstant %int 2
%int_3 = OpConstant %int 3
%int_4 = OpConstant %int 4
%int_5 = OpConstant %int 5
%uint_0 = OpConstant %uint 0
%uint_32 = OpConstant %uint 32
%v4f1_0_0_0 = OpConstantComposite %vec4 %float_0_0 %float_0_0 %float_0_0 %float_1_0
%v4f_0_2_0_5_0_8_1_0 = OpConstantComposite %vec4 %float_0_2 %float_0_5 %float_0_8 %float_1_0 ; Placeholder for final color components

; Global Variables
%worldPos = OpVariable %_ptr_Input_vec3 Input
%normal = OpVariable %_ptr_Input_vec3 Input
%uv = OpVariable %_ptr_Input_vec2 Input
%tangent = OpVariable %_ptr_Input_vec3 Input
%color = OpVariable %_ptr_Input_vec3 Input
%outColor = OpVariable %_ptr_Output_vec4 Output
%_ = OpVariable %_ptr_Uniform_UBO Uniform ; UBO instance

; Main function
%main = OpFunction %void None %_func_void
%entry = OpLabel
  ; Load UBO members
  %lightPosPtr = OpMemberAccess %_ptr_Uniform_vec4 %_ %int_3
  %lightPos = OpLoad %vec4 %lightPosPtr
  %cameraPosPtr = OpMemberAccess %_ptr_Uniform_vec4 %_ %int_4
  %cameraPos = OpLoad %vec4 %cameraPosPtr

  %lPos3 = OpVectorExtractDynamic %vec3 %lightPos %int_0 ; lightPos.xyz
  %cPos3 = OpVectorExtractDynamic %vec3 %cameraPos %int_0 ; cameraPos.xyz
  %wPos3 = OpLoad %vec3 %worldPos

  ; Normalize N, L, V
  %N_raw = OpLoad %vec3 %normal
  %N = OpNormalize %vec3 %N_raw

  %L_vec = OpFSub %vec3 %lPos3 %wPos3
  %L = OpNormalize %vec3 %L_vec

  %V_vec = OpFSub %vec3 %cPos3 %wPos3
  %V = OpNormalize %vec3 %V_vec

  ; Diffuse
  %dotNL = OpDot %float %N %L
  %diffuse_raw = OpExtInst %float %GLSLstd450 Max %dotNL %float_0_0

  ; Reflection (R = reflect(-L, N))
  %negL = OpFNegate %vec3 %L
  %R = OpExtInst %vec3 %GLSLstd450 Reflect %negL %N

  ; Specular
  %dotRV = OpDot %float %R %V
  %specular_raw = OpExtInst %float %GLSLstd450 Max %dotRV %float_0_0
  %specular = OpExtInst %float %GLSLstd450 Pow %specular_raw %uint_32

  ; Final grayscale intensity: 0.2 + 0.5*diffuse + 0.8*specular
  %term1 = OpFMul %float %float_0_5 %diffuse_raw
  %term2 = OpFMul %float %float_0_8 %specular
  %intensity = OpFAdd %float %float_0_2 %term1
  %intensity = OpFAdd %float %intensity %term2

  %outColor_val = OpCompositeConstruct %vec4 %intensity %intensity %intensity %float_1_0
  OpStore %outColor %outColor_val
  OpReturn
OpFunctionEnd

; SPIR-V
; Version: 1.0
; Generator: Google Shaderc over Glslang; 1
; Capabilities: Shader
; Memory Model: Logical GLSL450

OpCapability Shader
OpMemoryModel Logical GLSL450

; GLSL.std.450 Extended Instruction Set
%GLSLstd450 = OpExtInstImport "GLSL.std.450"

; Forward declarations (important for SPIR-V, as IDs must be defined before use)
OpDecorate %worldPos_in Location 0
OpDecorate %normal_in Location 1
OpDecorate %uv_in Location 2
OpDecorate %tangent_in Location 3
OpDecorate %color_in Location 4
OpDecorate %outColor_out Location 0

OpDecorate %UBO_struct Block
OpMemberDecorate %UBO_struct 0 Offset 0
OpMemberDecorate %UBO_struct 1 Offset 64
OpMemberDecorate %UBO_struct 2 Offset 128
OpMemberDecorate %UBO_struct 3 Offset 192
OpMemberDecorate %UBO_struct 4 Offset 208
OpMemberDecorate %UBO_struct 5 Offset 224

OpDecorate %ubo_var DescriptorSet 0
OpDecorate %ubo_var Binding 0

%void = OpTypeVoid
%bool = OpTypeBool
%float = OpTypeFloat 32
%int = OpTypeInt 32 0 ; Signed 32-bit integer
%uint = OpTypeInt 32 1 ; Unsigned 32-bit integer

%v2float = OpTypeVector %float 2
%v3float = OpTypeVector %float 3
%v4float = OpTypeVector %float 4

%mat4 = OpTypeMatrix %v4float 4

%UBO_struct = OpTypeStruct %mat4 %mat4 %mat4 %v4float %v4float %v4float

%_ptr_Function_void = OpTypeFunction %void

%_ptr_Input_v3float = OpTypePointer Input %v3float
%_ptr_Input_v2float = OpTypePointer Input %v2float
%_ptr_Output_v4float = OpTypePointer Output %v4float
%_ptr_Uniform_UBO_struct = OpTypePointer Uniform %UBO_struct
%_ptr_Function_float = OpTypePointer Function %float
%_ptr_Function_v3float = OpTypePointer Function %v3float
%_ptr_Function_v4float = OpTypePointer Function %v4float
%_ptr_Uniform_v4float = OpTypePointer Uniform %v4float ; Moved this up

; Constants
%float_0_0 = OpConstant %float 0.0
%float_1_0 = OpConstant %float 1.0
%float_0_1 = OpConstant %float 0.1
%float_0_6 = OpConstant %float 0.6
%float_0_8 = OpConstant %float 0.8
%float_64_0 = OpConstant %float 64.0

%int_0 = OpConstant %int 0
%int_1 = OpConstant %int 1
%int_2 = OpConstant %int 2
%int_3 = OpConstant %int 3
%int_4 = OpConstant %int 4
%int_5 = OpConstant %int 5

%v4float_1_1_1_1 = OpConstantComposite %v4float %float_1_0 %float_1_0 %float_1_0 %float_1_0

; Global variables
%worldPos_in = OpVariable %_ptr_Input_v3float Input
%normal_in = OpVariable %_ptr_Input_v3float Input
%uv_in = OpVariable %_ptr_Input_v2float Input
%tangent_in = OpVariable %_ptr_Input_v3float Input
%color_in = OpVariable %_ptr_Input_v3float Input

%outColor_out = OpVariable %_ptr_Output_v4float Output

%ubo_var = OpVariable %_ptr_Uniform_UBO_struct Uniform

OpEntryPoint Fragment %main "main" %worldPos_in %normal_in %uv_in %tangent_in %color_in %outColor_out %ubo_var

OpExecutionMode %main OriginUpperLeft

OpSource GLSL 450
OpName %main "main"
OpName %worldPos_in "worldPos"
OpName %normal_in "normal"
OpName %uv_in "uv"
OpName %tangent_in "tangent"
OpName %color_in "color"
OpName %outColor_out "outColor"
OpName %UBO_struct "UBO"
OpMemberName %UBO_struct 0 "model"
OpMemberName %UBO_struct 1 "view"
OpMemberName %UBO_struct 2 "projection"
OpMemberName %UBO_struct 3 "lightPos"
OpMemberName %UBO_struct 4 "cameraPos"
OpMemberName %UBO_struct 5 "params"
OpName %ubo_var "UBO_Instance"

%main = OpFunction %void None %_ptr_Function_void
%entry = OpLabel
    ; Load Input Variables
    %worldPos_val = OpLoad %v3float %worldPos_in
    %normal_val = OpLoad %v3float %normal_in

    ; Access UBO members
    %lightPos_ptr = OpAccessChain %_ptr_Uniform_v4float %ubo_var %int_3
    %lightPos_val = OpLoad %v4float %lightPos_ptr

    %cameraPos_ptr = OpAccessChain %_ptr_Uniform_v4float %ubo_var %int_4
    %cameraPos_val = OpLoad %v4float %cameraPos_ptr

    ; Normalize normal_val (N)
    %N = OpNormalize %v3float %normal_val

    ; Calculate Light Direction (L)
    %lightPos_xyz = OpVectorShuffle %v3float %lightPos_val %lightPos_val 0 1 2 ; Extract xyz from lightPos_val
    %lightDir_unnormalized = OpFSub %v3float %lightPos_xyz %worldPos_val
    %L = OpNormalize %v3float %lightDir_unnormalized

    ; Calculate View Direction (V)
    %cameraPos_xyz = OpVectorShuffle %v3float %cameraPos_val %cameraPos_val 0 1 2 ; Extract xyz from cameraPos_val
    %viewDir_unnormalized = OpFSub %v3float %cameraPos_xyz %worldPos_val
    %V = OpNormalize %v3float %viewDir_unnormalized

    ; Calculate Half-Vector (H)
    %L_plus_V = OpFAdd %v3float %L %V
    %H = OpNormalize %v3float %L_plus_V

    ; Calculate Diffuse: max(dot(N, L), 0.0)
    %N_dot_L = OpDot %float %N %L
    %diffuse_unclamped = OpExtInst %float %GLSLstd450 FMax %N_dot_L %float_0_0
    %diffuse = %diffuse_unclamped

    ; Calculate Specular: pow(max(dot(N, H), 0.0), 64.0)
    %N_dot_H = OpDot %float %N %H
    %specular_unclamped = OpExtInst %float %GLSLstd450 FMax %N_dot_H %float_0_0
    %specular = OpExtInst %float %GLSLstd450 Pow %specular_unclamped %float_64_0

    ; Calculate Grayscale Intensity: 0.1 + 0.6 * diffuse + 0.8 * specular
    %term1 = OpFMul %float %float_0_6 %diffuse
    %term2 = OpFMul %float %float_0_8 %specular
    %sum_terms = OpFAdd %float %term1 %term2
    %intensity = OpFAdd %float %float_0_1 %sum_terms

    ; Output Color: vec4(intensity, intensity, intensity, 1.0)
    %outColor_vec3 = OpCompositeConstruct %v3float %intensity %intensity %intensity
    %final_outColor = OpCompositeConstruct %v4float %intensity %intensity %intensity %float_1_0
    OpStore %outColor_out %final_outColor

OpReturn
OpFunctionEnd

%_ptr_Input_v3float = OpTypePointer Input %v3float
%_ptr_Input_v2float = OpTypePointer Input %v2float
%_ptr_Input_v4float = OpTypePointer Input %v4float
%_ptr_Output_v4float = OpTypePointer Output %v4float
%_ptr_Function_v3float = OpTypePointer Function %v3float
%_ptr_Function_float = OpTypePointer Function %float
%_ptr_Function_v4float = OpTypePointer Function %v4float
%_ptr_Uniform_v4float = OpTypePointer Uniform %v4float

%uint = OpTypeInt 32 0
%uint_0 = OpConstant %uint 0
%uint_3 = OpConstant %uint 3
%uint_4 = OpConstant %uint 4

%UBO = OpTypeStruct %mat4v4float %mat4v4float %mat4v4float %v4float %v4float %v4float
%_ptr_Uniform_UBO = OpTypePointer Uniform %UBO
%_ptr_Uniform_mat4v4float = OpTypePointer Uniform %mat4v4float
%_ptr_Uniform_v4float_3 = OpTypePointer Uniform %v4float

; Constant Declarations
%_float_0 = OpConstant %float 0.0
%_float_1 = OpConstant %float 1.0
%_float_3 = OpConstant %float 3.0
%_float_0_2 = OpConstant %float 0.2
%_float_0_3 = OpConstant %float 0.3
%_float_0_8 = OpConstant %float 0.8
%_float_0_5 = OpConstant %float 0.5
%_float_1_0_tint = OpConstant %float 1.0
%_float_0_8_tint = OpConstant %float 0.8
%_float_0_5_tint = OpConstant %float 0.5
%v3float_0_2_0_3_0_8 = OpConstantComposite %v3float %_float_0_2 %_float_0_3 %_float_0_8
%v3float_1_0_0_8_0_5 = OpConstantComposite %v3float %_float_1_0_tint %_float_0_8_tint %_float_0_5_tint

; Global Variables
%worldPos = OpVariable %_ptr_Input_v3float Input
%normal = OpVariable %_ptr_Input_v3float Input
%uv = OpVariable %_ptr_Input_v2float Input
%tangent = OpVariable %_ptr_Input_v3float Input
%color = OpVariable %_ptr_Input_v3float Input
%outColor = OpVariable %_ptr_Output_v4float Output
%_DescriptorSet0_Binding0_UBO = OpVariable %_ptr_Uniform_UBO Uniform

; Function Declarations
%main_func = OpTypeFunction %void

; Main Function
%main = OpFunction %void None %main_func
%main_label = OpLabel
  ; Get input normal
  %in_normal_v3 = OpLoad %v3float %normal

  ; Normalize normal (N)
  %N_v3 = OpNormalize %v3float %in_normal_v3

  ; Get worldPos
  %in_worldPos_v3 = OpLoad %v3float %worldPos

  ; Get cameraPos from UBO
  %cameraPos_ptr = OpAccessChain %_ptr_Uniform_v4float %_DescriptorSet0_Binding0_UBO %uint_4
  %cameraPos_v4 = OpLoad %v4float %cameraPos_ptr
  %cameraPos_v3 = OpVectorExtractDynamic %v3float %cameraPos_v4 %uint_0 ; Assuming xyz

  ; Calculate View vector (V) = cameraPos.xyz - worldPos
  %V_v3_unnormalized = OpFSub %v3float %cameraPos_v3 %in_worldPos_v3
  %V_v3 = OpNormalize %v3float %V_v3_unnormalized

  ; Calculate dot(N,V)
  %dotNV = OpDot %float %N_v3 %V_v3

  ; Calculate max(dot(N,V), 0)
  %max_dotNV_0 = OpSNativeMax %float %dotNV %_float_0

  ; Calculate (1 - max(dot(N,V), 0))
  %one_minus_max_dotNV_0 = OpFSub %float %_float_1 %max_dotNV_0

  ; Calculate rim = pow(1 - max(dot(N,V),0), 3)
  %rim = OpPow %float %one_minus_max_dotNV_0 %_float_3

  ; Get lightPos from UBO
  %lightPos_ptr = OpAccessChain %_ptr_Uniform_v4float %_DescriptorSet0_Binding0_UBO %uint_3
  %lightPos_v4 = OpLoad %v4float %lightPos_ptr
  %lightPos_v3 = OpVectorExtractDynamic %v3float %lightPos_v4 %uint_0 ; Assuming xyz

  ; Calculate Light vector (L) = lightPos.xyz - worldPos
  %L_v3_unnormalized = OpFSub %v3float %lightPos_v3 %in_worldPos_v3
  %L_v3 = OpNormalize %v3float %L_v3_unnormalized

  ; Calculate diffuse = max(dot(N,L), 0)
  %dotNL = OpDot %float %N_v3 %L_v3
  %diffuse = OpSNativeMax %float %dotNL %_float_0

  ; Calculate base color (0.2, 0.3, 0.8)
  %baseColor_v3 = OpLoad %v3float %v3float_0_2_0_3_0_8

  ; Calculate (0.3 + 0.5*diffuse)
  %term_0_5_diffuse = OpFMul %float %_float_0_5 %diffuse
  %factor_0_3_plus_0_5_diffuse = OpFAdd %float %_float_0_3 %term_0_5_diffuse

  ; Calculate modulatedBaseColor = base color * (0.3 + 0.5*diffuse)
  %modulatedBaseColor_v3 = OpVectorTimesScalar %v3float %baseColor_v3 %factor_0_3_plus_0_5_diffuse

  ; Calculate rim tint (1.0, 0.8, 0.5) * rim
  %rimTintBase_v3 = OpLoad %v3float %v3float_1_0_0_8_0_5
  %rimTint_v3 = OpVectorTimesScalar %v3float %rimTintBase_v3 %rim

  ; Calculate finalColor = modulatedBaseColor + rimTint
  %finalColor_v3 = OpFAdd %v3float %modulatedBaseColor_v3 %rimTint_v3

  ; Output alpha 1.0
  %outAlpha = OpLoad %float %_float_1

  ; Compose outColor
  %finalColor_v4 = OpCompositeConstruct %v4float %finalColor_v3 %outAlpha

  ; Store outColor
  OpStore %outColor %finalColor_v4
  OpReturn
OpFunctionEnd

; SPIR-V
; Version: 1.0
; Generator: Google Shaderc over GLSL; 
; Capabilities: Shader
OpCapability Shader
OpExtInstImport %GLSLstd450 "GLSL.std.450"
OpMemoryModel Logical GLSL450
OpEntryPoint Fragment %main "main" %worldPos_in %normal_in %uv_in %tangent_in %color_in %outColor_out %ubo_instance
OpExecutionMode %main OriginUpperLeft
OpSource GLSL 450
OpName %main "main"
OpName %worldPos_in "worldPos"
OpName %normal_in "normal"
OpName %uv_in "uv"
OpName %tangent_in "tangent"
OpName %color_in "color"
OpName %outColor_out "outColor"
OpName %UBO "UBO"
OpMemberName %UBO 0 "model"
OpMemberName %UBO 1 "view"
OpMemberName %UBO 2 "projection"
OpMemberName %UBO 3 "lightPos"
OpMemberName %UBO 4 "cameraPos"
OpMemberName %UBO 5 "params"
OpName %ubo_instance "ubo_instance"
OpName %worldPos_var "worldPos_var"
OpName %normal_var "normal_var"
OpName %uv_var "uv_var"
OpName %checker_val_var "checker_val_var"
OpName %lightDir_var "lightDir_var"
OpName %diffuse_var "diffuse_var"
OpName %lambertian_factor_var "lambertian_factor_var"


; Decorations for Input/Output
OpDecorate %worldPos_in Location 0
OpDecorate %normal_in Location 1
OpDecorate %uv_in Location 2
OpDecorate %tangent_in Location 3
OpDecorate %color_in Location 4
OpDecorate %outColor_out Location 0

; Decorations for UBO
OpDecorate %UBO Block
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 0 ColMajor
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 0 Offset 0
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 0 MatrixStride 16
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 1 ColMajor
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 1 Offset 64
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 1 MatrixStride 16
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 2 ColMajor
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 2 Offset 128
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 2 MatrixStride 16
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 3 Offset 192
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 4 Offset 208
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 5 Offset 224
OpDecorate %ubo_instance DescriptorSet 0
OpDecorate %ubo_instance Binding 0

; Types
%void = OpTypeVoid
%bool = OpTypeBool
%float = OpTypeFloat 32
%int = OpTypeInt 32 1 ; Signed integer
%uint = OpTypeInt 32 0 ; Unsigned integer

%v2float = OpTypeVector %float 2
%v3float = OpTypeVector %float 3
%v4float = OpTypeVector %float 4
%m4v4float = OpTypeMatrix %v4float 4 ; mat4

; UBO structure
%UBO = OpTypeStruct %m4v4float %m4v4float %m4v4float %v4float %v4float %v4float

; Pointer types
%ptr_Input_v3float = OpTypePointer Input %v3float
%ptr_Input_v2float = OpTypePointer Input %v2float
%ptr_Output_v4float = OpTypePointer Output %v4float
%ptr_Uniform_UBO = OpTypePointer Uniform %UBO
%ptr_Uniform_v4float = OpTypePointer Uniform %v4float ; Pointer to vec4 within UBO
%ptr_Function_v2float = OpTypePointer Function %v2float
%ptr_Function_float = OpTypePointer Function %float
%ptr_Function_v3float = OpTypePointer Function %v3float
%ptr_Function_v4float = OpTypePointer Function %v4float

%void_func = OpTypeFunction %void

; Constants
%float_0 = OpConstant %float 0.0
%float_0_2 = OpConstant %float 0.2
%float_0_5 = OpConstant %float 0.5
%float_0_9 = OpConstant %float 0.9
%float_1 = OpConstant %float 1.0
%float_8 = OpConstant %float 8.0
%int_0 = OpConstant %int 0
%int_1 = OpConstant %int 1
%int_2 = OpConstant %int 2
%uint_0 = OpConstant %uint 0
%uint_3 = OpConstant %uint 3 ; Index for lightPos in UBO

; Global variables
%worldPos_in = OpVariable %ptr_Input_v3float Input
%normal_in = OpVariable %ptr_Input_v3float Input
%uv_in = OpVariable %ptr_Input_v2float Input
%tangent_in = OpVariable %ptr_Input_v3float Input
%color_in = OpVariable %ptr_Input_v3float Input
%outColor_out = OpVariable %ptr_Output_v4float Output
%ubo_instance = OpVariable %ptr_Uniform_UBO Uniform

; Main function
%main = OpFunction %void None %void_func
%label_main = OpLabel
  ; Function-scope variables
  %worldPos_var = OpVariable %ptr_Function_v3float Function
  %normal_var = OpVariable %ptr_Function_v3float Function
  %uv_var = OpVariable %ptr_Function_v2float Function
  %checker_val_var = OpVariable %ptr_Function_float Function
  %lightDir_var = OpVariable %ptr_Function_v3float Function
  %diffuse_var = OpVariable %ptr_Function_float Function
  %lambertian_factor_var = OpVariable %ptr_Function_float Function

  ; Load input variables
  %100 = OpLoad %v3float %worldPos_in
  OpStore %worldPos_var %100
  %101 = OpLoad %v3float %normal_in
  OpStore %normal_var %101
  %102 = OpLoad %v2float %uv_in
  OpStore %uv_var %102

  ; Checkerboard calculation
  %103 = OpLoad %v2float %uv_var
  %104 = OpVectorTimesScalar %v2float %103 %float_8
  %105 = OpCompositeExtract %float %104 0
  %106 = OpCompositeExtract %float %104 1
  %107 = OpExtInst %float %GLSLstd450 Floor %105
  %108 = OpExtInst %float %GLSLstd450 Floor %106
  %109 = OpFAdd %float %107 %108
  %110 = OpConvertFToS %int %109
  %111 = OpSMod %int %110 %int_2
  %112 = OpIEqual %bool %111 %int_0
  %113 = OpSelect %float %112 %float_0_9 %float_0_2
  OpStore %checker_val_var %113

  ; Lambertian shading calculation
  %114 = OpLoad %v3float %normal_var
  %115 = OpExtInst %v3float %GLSLstd450 Normalize %114
  %116 = OpAccessChain %ptr_Uniform_v4float %ubo_instance %uint_3
  %117 = OpLoad %v4float %116
  %118 = OpVectorShuffle %v3float %117 %117 0 1 2 ; Extract xyz from lightPos (v4 to v3)
  %119 = OpLoad %v3float %worldPos_var
  %120 = OpFSub %v3float %118 %119 ; lightDir = lightPos.xyz - worldPos
  %121 = OpExtInst %v3float %GLSLstd450 Normalize %120 ; Normalized light direction
  OpStore %lightDir_var %121

  %122 = OpDot %float %115 %121 ; Diffuse factor
  %123 = OpExtInst %float %GLSLstd450 FMax %float_0 %122 ; Clamp to 0
  %124 = OpExtInst %float %GLSLstd450 FMin %float_1 %123 ; Clamp to 1
  OpStore %diffuse_var %124

  %125 = OpFMul %float %float_0_5 %124 ; 0.5 * diffuse
  %126 = OpFAdd %float %float_0_5 %125 ; 0.5 + 0.5 * diffuse
  OpStore %lambertian_factor_var %126

  ; Combine and output
  %127 = OpLoad %float %checker_val_var
  %128 = OpLoad %float %lambertian_factor_var
  %129 = OpFMul %float %127 %128 ; final_color_channel = checker_val * lambertian_factor
  %130 = OpCompositeConstruct %v4float %129 %129 %129 %float_1 ; RGB from final_color_channel, A=1.0
  OpStore %outColor_out %130

  OpReturn
OpFunctionEnd
